food_subgroup: Toasted wheat vermicelli in coconut milk and sugar with ghee-fried nuts

ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has no specific concept for Semiya Payasam. Nearest parent is a vermicelli milk dessert. A new concept for South Indian payasam preparations is recommended.
ontology.foodex2_description: Vermicelli in coconut milk/milk dessert with sugar, South Indian (closest parent)

serving.natural_language_portion: A standard serving of semiya payasam is 1 small bowl (approximately 150g) — a warm, slightly thick, creamy dessert soup of thin wheat vermicelli in sweetened milk and coconut milk, garnished with golden cashews and plump raisins fried in ghee.

nutrient_source.title: Payasam (Semiya) — ICMR-NIN Nutritive Value of Indian Foods, Entry EX000079
nutrient_source.organization: Indian Council of Medical Research — National Institute of Nutrition (ICMR-NIN)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Exact match in ICMR-NIN under entry EX000079 — Semiya Payasam (vermicelli in coconut milk and milk), standard South Indian preparation.

gi_source: University of Sydney GI Database — estimated from wheat vermicelli in full-fat coconut milk and cow's milk; coconut milk fat and cow's milk protein moderate the GI of the vermicelli.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium approximately 83mg per bowl (150g) — very low. Payasam is naturally very low in sodium.
hypertension.guidance: Suitable for hypertensive patients from a sodium standpoint. The moderate sugar and fat content are the primary dietary considerations.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Moderate GI (~62); glycaemic load per bowl (150g) is approximately 27. The sugar content (16.5g/100g) combined with wheat vermicelli creates a significant glycaemic load.
type2_diabetes.guidance: Diabetic patients should limit payasam to half a serving (75g) or choose a reduced-sugar version. The coconut milk fat moderates the GI somewhat but the sugar content is a significant concern.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Coconut milk provides lauric acid with anti-inflammatory properties; cardamom and saffron have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties; milk provides calcium.
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Suitable in moderation for patients with rheumatoid arthritis. The coconut milk and spice content provide some anti-inflammatory benefit. Contains gluten (wheat vermicelli) — not suitable for coeliac patients.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Semiya Payasam is a classic South Indian festival dessert — thin wheat vermicelli (semiya) toasted in ghee until golden, then simmered in a mixture of full-fat cow's milk and coconut milk, sweetened with sugar, and flavoured with cardamom and saffron. Finished with ghee-fried cashews and raisins. The result is a creamy, fragrant, slightly thick dessert soup served warm or at room temperature. Payasam differs from North Indian kheer in using coconut milk and wheat vermicelli rather than pure cow's milk and rice. A traditional Onam Sadya and festive offering in South India and Singapore's Tamil community.

confidence_reason: Exact match found in ICMR-NIN under entry EX000079 — Semiya Payasam, standard South Indian preparation.
notes: Contains gluten (wheat vermicelli), dairy (milk, ghee), and tree nuts (cashew). Not vegan, not gluten-free, not dairy-free. Cholesterol approximately 18mg per bowl (150g). Key distinction from Kheer (#095): payasam uses coconut milk (higher saturated fat), wheat vermicelli (not rice), and the texture is slightly looser. Higher saturated fat than kheer due to coconut milk MCTs.
